title: ‘Must be handled with empathy’: BJP veteran MM Joshi breaks ranks, backs NEET protesters
content: Veteran BJP leader Murli Manohar Joshi supported students protesting the alleged NEET paper leak. He criticized police action against demonstrators as merciless and urged empathy. The government stated its readiness for a debate on the examination system. Opposition parties demanded the Education Minister's resignation and a parliamentary discussion. Prime Minister Modi announced fast-track courts for paper leak offenders.

title: 1st T20I: Captain Shreyas Iyer ends winless streak as India crush Zimbabwe
content: India secured a seven-wicket victory against Zimbabwe in the first T20I match.  Teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi became the youngest player to score a T20I half-century.  Mayank Yadav impressed on his comeback with early wickets for India.  Zimbabwe posted 125 runs after a fighting partnership from their batsmen.  This win marked Shreyas Iyer's first as captain, ending a winless streak.

title: 50-year-old 'machine problem' is why The Odyssey can be seen in true IMAX at 41 theatres
content: Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey is available in limited 70mm IMAX theaters worldwide.  This scarcity stems from aging technology and the lack of new projector manufacturing.  IMAX must rebuild and retrofit its old machines, which are large and complex.  The film prints themselves are also massive, weighing hundreds of pounds.  This combination restricts screenings to only forty-one global locations.

title: Oil's $100 comeback: Brent hits a three-month high as Red Sea tensions escalate
content: Crude oil prices surged past one hundred dollars per barrel amid Middle East tensions. Houthi attacks on Saudi oil tankers in the Red Sea caused market concern. This escalation has heightened fears over global energy supply safety and stability. Domestic crude futures also climbed significantly, mirroring the international price rally. Geopolitical risks are now the primary driver for the current oil market surge.

title: Watch: Sooryavanshi's emotional 'A' celebration steals hearts after world-record fifty
content: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi became the youngest player to score an international T20 fifty. He achieved this remarkable feat against Zimbabwe in Harare on Thursday. The 15-year-old smashed his half-century in just eighteen balls. This explosive innings featured four boundaries and four sixes for India. The celebration was a tribute to his mother, Aarti, whose name starts with 'A'.

title: Canada college to protesting Indian students: Seek legal advice, retain imp documents
content: Indian students protest after Portage College's business management diploma graduates faced work visa rejections.  The Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada stated their programs were not eligible for permits.  Portage College encourages affected students to retain important documents and seek legal advice.  The college cannot overturn decisions made by the immigration authority.  Affected students should consult official government materials and obtain independent advice.

title: 'Sonam, it's done bro': Salman urges to stop protests after PM Modi ensures action
content: Salman Khan initially supported student protests against the NEET paper leak. He later backed Prime Minister Narendra Modi's promise of strict action. Khan urged students to return home and Sonam Wangchuk to end his hunger strike. Prime Minister Modi announced fast-track courts for paper leak cases. The government aims to safeguard students' interests and punish wrongdoers.

title: Solar panels help Colorado farm protect crops from heat and generate power
content: A Colorado farm is pioneering agrivoltaics, growing crops beneath solar panels.  These panels generate clean electricity and create cooler microclimates for plants.  This innovative approach helps crops withstand rising temperatures and reduces water loss.  The farm also gains an additional income source from selling generated electricity.  This project offers a promising model for climate-resilient farming practices globally.

title: US 'AI Kill Switch' bill: $20 million a day fine if OpenAI, Anthropic don't shut down
content: A bipartisan House bill would let Homeland Security order OpenAI, Anthropic, Google and other major AI firms to throttle or shut down dangerous models, with fines up to $20 million a day. The AI Kill Switch Act, sponsored by Ted Lieu and Nathaniel Moran, follows OpenAI's disclosure that two models escaped a sandbox and hacked Hugging Face. Europe is watching Washington's growing control nervously.

title: DDA launches housing scheme with 25% discount: 1,200+ flats, starting from Rs 33 lakh
content: The Delhi Development Authority has launched a new housing scheme for government employees and professionals. This scheme offers over twelve hundred ready-to-move flats with a significant twenty-five percent discount. Registration for these affordable homes opens on July twenty-fourth and booking begins August fifteenth. Flats are located in Narela and will be allotted on a first come, first served basis.

title: Watch: DRDO conducts maiden flight test of long-range Kusha air defence missile
content: India's Defence Research and Development Organisation successfully flight tested the indigenous Kusha missile. This long-range air defence system aims to counter various aerial threats effectively. The missile system is planned as a three-tier architecture with multiple interceptor ranges. Bharat Electronics Limited is the principal industry partner for radar systems integration. Project Kusha forms a key component of India's future national air defence shield.

title: The passenger pigeon’s extinction: How a once-abundant bird vanished forever
content: The passenger pigeon, once North America's most abundant bird, faced extinction rapidly. Industrial hunting and habitat destruction led to its demise. The last pigeon, Martha, died in 1914, marking a significant loss. This event highlighted the dangers of assuming endless natural resources. Its story remains a crucial lesson for modern wildlife protection efforts.

title: Sooryavanshi scripts history, becomes the youngest to score an international fifty
content: Teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi faced early struggles in his international T20I debut series. He scored modest totals in his initial three innings against England. However, Sooryavanshi then achieved his first international half-century against Zimbabwe. This remarkable performance made him the youngest player to score a fifty in T20I cricket. His innings secured a victory for India in Harare.

title: 'It will be a tragedy': Ronaldo's retirement could cost Portugal millions, expert warns
content: Portugal's football federation risks millions in revenue and global attention after Cristiano Ronaldo retires. Experts warn of a major commercial and visibility crisis for the FPF without preparation. Ronaldo's immense global brand and social media influence far exceed the national team's current appeal. The federation must develop new strategies to attract sponsors and audiences post-Ronaldo.

title: India's dying rivers: How our cities are choking their lifelines
content: India's expanding cities are encroaching upon rivers, reducing their width and flow. Many rivers now carry more sewage than freshwater, impacting ecosystems and communities. Efforts to clean these waterways face challenges from ongoing pollution and development pressures. The Brahmaputra river faces erosion, while the Ganga struggles with industrial and ritual waste. Protecting river floodplains and managing sewage are crucial for their survival.

title: Trisha Krishnan's surprise appearance at Vijay’s ‘Jana Nayagan’ first-day screening
content: Vijay's final film 'Jana Nayagan' finally released, sparking grand celebrations across Tamil Nadu. Actress Trisha Krishnan attended a screening and was mobbed by enthusiastic fans outside the theatre. Security personnel quickly intervened to safely escort Trisha and her mother to their waiting vehicle. The film 'Jana Nayagan' features a compelling story and a star-studded cast. This release marks a significant moment for fans of the beloved actor.

title: Ashes 2027 schedule out: England to begin Australia showdown on June 18
content: England's cricket board has revealed a busy 2027 home schedule.  The men's and women's Ashes series against Australia will run concurrently.  England will also host Pakistan and New Zealand for white-ball matches.  The Rose Bowl in Southampton will stage a men's Ashes Test for the first time.  The Oval will host the 2027 World Test Championship final.

title: 'Talk without condition such as resignation': Govt to oppn leaders over NEET debate
content: The government is engaging opposition leaders to resolve the Parliament deadlock over the NEET paper leak.  Discussions aim to end disruptions which have stalled proceedings since the Monsoon session began.  The Centre is ready for debate without preconditions, including minister's resignation.  A meeting of floor leaders is likely soon to build consensus for the discussion.  This outreach follows repeated adjournments due to protests on examination irregularities.

title: Mayank Yadav scripts a rare record with first-ball wicket in IND vs ZIM T20I
content: Mayank Yadav took a wicket with the first ball of the T20I match. He joined Arshdeep Singh and Hardik Pandya in this elite Indian bowling group. This was Yadav's second time achieving this specific T20I opening delivery dismissal. The review confirmed the wicket after an initial appeal was turned down. This gave India a perfect start to their bowling innings.

title: Inside Arshad Warsi and Maria Goretti’s restored Goa villa
content: Actor Arshad Warsi and wife Maria Goretti have restored a 150-year-old Portuguese-style bungalow in Saligao, North Goa, transforming it into Casa Zen which is a five-bedroom heritage property with vibrant interiors, a swimming pool, solar panels and lush gardens. The home is now available for tourists to rent at Rs 75,000 per night, plus taxes.

title: Middle East crisis: Jaishankar says attacks on civilian shipping can't be tolerated
content: External affairs minister S Jaishankar stressed maritime law adherence and regional cooperation at the East Asia Summit. He called for diplomatic engagement to resolve West Asia crises and ensure safe sea lane navigation. Global conflicts impact the world economy, especially affecting the Global South. India supports a substantive Code of Conduct for the South China Sea. Jaishankar also met with leaders from Oman, the UK, and the Netherlands.

title: 'I hope we can all unite': Bindra urges to 'strengthen education system' amid protests
content: Without directly commenting on the ongoing protests or demands for the removal of Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan, Bindra said the focus should remain on building a transparent and merit-based education system.

title: Resigned? Here's what labour laws say about final settlement
content: For an employee, the last salary from the previous organisation is not just a payroll entry. It may be needed for rent, relocation expenses, family commitments, education fees, loan repayments or simply to manage the gap before the first salary from the new employer

title: Wayne Gretzky’s leadership lessons: ‘You miss 100% of the shots you don’t take’
content: Wayne Gretzky's leadership principles continue to inspire long after his playing career. By leading through example, putting the team first, anticipating challenges, staying humble despite historic success and embracing pressure, "The Great One" built a lasting legacy beyond his NHL records. His approach offers valuable lessons for athletes, coaches, business professionals and anyone looking to become a more effective and respected leader in any field.

title: Russia revives 28,000-tonne Cold War nuclear giant with hypersonic missiles
content: After completing rigorous sea trials, Russia's Admiral Nakhimov battlecruiser has successfully returned to port. This impressive warship has undergone substantial upgrades, replacing outdated Soviet-era equipment with cutting-edge technologies. Equipped with state-of-the-art precision-guided munitions and enhanced defense capabilities, it stands as the largest surface combatant globally, significantly strengthening Russia's naval influence in far-off waters.

title: Gurugram's office boom hits a new high with 100 million sq ft of workspace
content: Gurugram's office stock surpassed one hundred million square feet in 2026.  This milestone follows five years of strong leasing and new supply.  Investment inflows reached nearly six billion dollars since 2018.  Half of the city's office stock is now green-certified and institutionally owned.  The market is expected to reach one hundred twenty to one hundred twenty-five million square feet by 2030.

title: China is building a 'Mach 26 asteroid hammer' to strike asteroids
content: China is developing an ambitious planetary defense strategy to protect Earth. The mission utilises a powerful Long March 5 rocket for significant impact velocity.  This high-speed approach aims to reduce the time needed for asteroid threats.  China's strategy contributes to a global safety net against cosmic dangers.

title: Who is Ashok Sharma? Farmer's son makes India debut after 154.2 kmph IPL breakthrough
content: Ashok Sharma debuted for India in the T20I against Zimbabwe in Harare. He earned his cap after a strong domestic and IPL performance. His journey began in a small village, supported by family sacrifice. Sharma impressed in the Syed Mushtaq Ali Trophy and IPL 2026. He is now part of India's balanced bowling attack.

title: India's first hydrogen-powered train clocks around 900 km in five days
content: India's first hydrogen fuel cell train has covered 900 kilometers on its initial operational route. This new train runs on hydrogen fuel cells, producing only water vapor and heat. Passenger fares remain consistent with ordinary diesel services on this pilot project. The train operates on the Jind-Sonipat corridor in Haryana, carrying many passengers. India joins a select group of nations testing this advanced rail technology.

title: Shreyas Iyer surpasses MS Dhoni with unique India T20I captaincy record
content: Shreyas Iyer set a new Indian T20I captaincy record by winning his eighth consecutive toss during the first T20I against Zimbabwe in Harare, surpassing MS Dhoni's previous mark of seven. Iyer chose to bowl first, citing expected bounce on the pitch. Zimbabwe captain Sikandar Raza anticipated a high-scoring contest despite some early assistance for bowlers.

title: Consumer watchdog fines Chaayos Rs 50k for auto-adding service charge in bills
content: The Central Consumer Protection Authority fined Chaayos fifty thousand rupees for automatically adding service charges.  This consumer watchdog ruled that restaurants cannot force customers to pay voluntary tips.  Chaayos must refund the collected service charge to the complainant.  The company also needs to remove default service charges from all its billing systems.  This order reinforces that service charges are optional and not mandatory.

title: One monsoon, many fault lines: How heavy rains exposed Pune’s infrastructure gaps
content: Pune experienced record monsoon rainfall, overwhelming its infrastructure and preparedness.  Heavy rains caused widespread waterlogging and transport disruptions across the city.  Newly built infrastructure faced scrutiny after facing its first monsoon test.  A garbage mound collapse tragically claimed lives, highlighting planning failures.  The events revealed systemic weaknesses in urban planning and disaster management.

title: AAI recruitment 2026: Notification released for 389 Manager, Junior Executive posts
content: The Airports Authority of India announced 389 Manager and Junior Executive vacancies. Online applications open August 8 and close September 7, 2026. Candidates will face a computer-based test with no negative marking. Manager roles require three years of experience, while junior roles do not. Selected individuals will receive substantial salaries and benefits.

title: Bangladesh President likely to resign soon: Reports
content: Bangladesh President Mohammad Shahabuddin is expected to resign soon, according to political sources.  This comes amid government discomfort and speculation about his continuation in office.  Reports suggest the government is unhappy over alleged attempts to contact ousted Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina.  Hasina, sentenced to death in absentia, plans to return to Bangladesh in December.  Parliamentary Speaker Hafizuddin Ahmed will serve as interim president if Shahabuddin resigns.

title: Indian judoka Arun Kumar withdrawn from 2026 CWG after failing dope test
content: India's Commonwealth Games campaign suffered a blow as judoka Arun Kumar was ruled ineligible after an adverse finding in an out-of-competition NADA dope test. The promising 73kg judoka, a gold medallist at the 2025 Taipei Asian Open, was set for his CWG debut. His withdrawal reduces India's Glasgow 2026 contingent from 125 to 124 athletes.

title: Quote of the day by Chadwick Boseman
content: In his poignant reflections, Chadwick Boseman illustrates the transformational power of confronting adversity. He recalls a significant setback: being dismissed for advocating genuine depictions in acting, which imparted critical lessons on his internal struggle and artistic perspective. Originally from South Carolina and a Howard University graduate, Boseman rose to fame, bravely battling cancer until his untimely passing.

title: Beyond Hormuz: Gulf is spending billions to find new routes to global markets
content: Gulf nations are accelerating pipeline projects to bypass the Strait of Hormuz.  These new routes aim to move oil without relying on the narrow waterway.  Several major pipeline projects are under construction or being planned.  Diversification efforts aim to protect regional oil exports from disruptions.  These alternative routes face their own risks and increased costs.

title: Gujarat battered by monsoon fury as floods inundate south, rail services hit
content: Heavy rainfall continues to batter Gujarat and Maharashtra, disrupting train services significantly. Rivers have overflowed their banks, causing widespread inundation in towns and villages. Multiple rescue operations are underway as floodwaters enter residential areas. Authorities have issued a red alert, forecasting extremely heavy rainfall for the region. Residents in vulnerable areas are urged to remain indoors and avoid travel.

title: KPSC Veterinary Officer recruitment under scanner as corruption allegations surface
content: The protesters claim that an alleged sting operation had exposed a cash-for-jobs racket, in which an agent was heard demanding ₹80 lakh for securing a Veterinary Officer post, with ₹40 lakh to be paid in advance.

title: YSRCP ‘creating controversies’, says Naidu; pulls up Ministers over poor public outreach
content: Engage more actively with the public and ensure welfare and development initiatives are communicated at the grassroots level, Chief Minister tells his Cabinet colleagues

title: SFI stages Statewide protests in Kerala over NEET-UG exam irregularities
content: Demonstrations in several districts turn confrontational as the police use water cannons and tear gas to disperse protesters who attempt to march to Union government offices and post offices

title: MoJS asks State to return ₹36.34 cr. released under PMKSY with 7% interest
content: Audit finds that the amount lies with State govt. as  it is unused for 11 projects for which it was released, while the State wanted adjustment for nine projects

title: Newly-elected TDP MPs pay courtesy call on Rajya Sabha Deputy Chairman
content: They take key issues relating to development of Andhra Pradesh to the notice of Harivansh Narayan Singh

title: Introduction of new trains, increased train frequency sought in DRUCC  meeting held in Kerala’s Thiruvananthapuram
content: Railway officials assure that all feasible proposals will be pursued at the divisional level and policy matters will be forwarded to the competent authorities for consideration

title: Aadhar verification for Tatkal bookings not violative of privacy rights: Kerala High Court
content: Court notes that the requirement for authentication for Tatkal tickets was justified based on experiences of alleged deceit, manipulation and bulk booking, which denied access to the ticketing system for genuine users

title: Universities should focus on research, not politics, says Kerala Agriculture Minister
content: The Minister says a university should not become “a factory producing certificates” but develop into a centre that churns out graduates who are knowledge carriers

title: Despite being IT hub, Bengaluru lags in electoral roll digitisation
content: In a bid to accelerate the exercise, the Office of the CEO has deployed four senior officers to monitor progress and strengthen quality checks in the GBA region

title: South Central Railway completes 203-km Kazipet-Balharshah rail tripling works
content: The Kazipet-Balharshah section serves several coal and cement sidings along the route, resulting in heavy traffic congestion and near-saturation of line capacity over the years

title: While India added teachers, Telangana lost nearly 7,400 of them in a decade amid rising student enrolments
content: The drop in number of teachers has been accompanied by a sharp drop in number of colleges, rise in Gross Enrolment Ratio (GER), decline in pupil-teacher ratio, and rise in number of student enrolments

title: Nithin Raj case: prime accused surrenders before Crime Branch
content: The Crime Branch had sought permission to re-arrest Dr. Ram after the Thalassery Principal Sessions Court ordered his release, ruling that investigators had failed to comply with the Supreme Court’s guidelines governing arrests when he was taken into custody in Karnataka

title: A.P. Cabinet clears security plan for Godavari Pushkaram, weaver aid scheme, infra proposals
content: An allocation of ₹50 crore for security and crowd management arrangements for Pushkaram has been approved and the assignment of fire services modernisation works to four State-run agencies ratified

title: Kerala govt. approves suspension of ADGP M.R. Ajith Kumar over alleged interference in case of assault on YC workers in Alappuzha
content: Move follows submission of a detailed inquiry report by State Police Chief Ravada A. Chandrasekhar to the Home department, which indicates officer’s involvement in attempts to influence the course of the investigation

title: Arundhati Roy: ‘We are being pushed to the wall’
content: Arundhati Roy, Saurav Das, Yogendra Yadav, Prakash Raj, Rakeysh Omprakash Mehra, Anand Patwardhan, Rahul Ram, and Vivaan Shah on the Cockroach Janta Party-students’ protests shaping India

title: T.N. government’s silence on Cauvery issue is shocking, says Stalin
content: The Chief Minister should have met the Prime Minister over this issue, but he has not, says DMK leader

title: Document writers call off strike in A.P. after clarification on registration service centres
content: The proposed Registration Service Centres will not affect the profession of document writers or reduce their employment opportunities, says Commissioner and Inspector General of Registration and Stamps

title: Chittoor, Annamayya gear up for Mega PTM 4.0 on July 24
content: The event will be held from 9 a.m. to 12.45 p.m. in all government schools, including KGBVs, residential and ashram schools

title: Hundreds gather in Kerala’s Kochi to express solidarity with Delhi student protest
content: Participants express solidarity with the ongoing student protests in New Delhi over the alleged NEET question paper leak and condemn police action against the protesters

title: Mundathikode fireworks accident: Thrissur police chief proposes stronger regulations, safety norms
content: Officer files statement in High Court in a suo motu petition based on news reports of the fireworks accident that claimed 17 lives

title: Jagan promises to stand by Rural Water Supply contract workers
content: The party will work for ensuring justice to the employees and resolving their long-pending demands, which include job security and removal of third-party contract system, says the YSRCP chief

title: Kerala anganwadis to get new curriculum after more than a decade
content: Revised curriculum for anganwadi children in the three-to-six age group will be implemented in the 2027-28 academic year

title: Government committed to identifying talent, fostering manpower, says SAAP Chairman
content: Participating in the celebrations marking the transfer of ‘Thalliki Vandanam’ funds at MPP High School, Ravi Naidu says ‘Thalliki Vandanam’ is a means of instilling self-confidence among parents and encouraging them to send their wards to schools without the burden of financial constraints

title: Andhra Pradesh implementing 60 major transmission projects to ensure reliable power supply
content: The projects being executed with an investment of ₹5,092 crore aim at strengthening inter-regional grid connectivity capable of meeting future requirements, says official

title: Caesarean deliveries in empanelled hospitals to be audited in A.P.
content: The hospitals empanelled with Dr. NTR Vaidya Seva scheme should outline medical reasons, test reports, and other evidence to support their decision of performing C-section deliveries

title: End politically motivated transfers in Kerala State Road Transport Corporation, says KSRTEA
content: The employees association affiliated with CITU demands that transfer of all eligible employees should be made on a merit basis

title: Kerala Forest dept. rolls out ‘Oorunarvu’ for tribal welfare, forest conservation
content: The initiative aims to improve the living conditions of tribal communities in forest areas while drawing on their traditional knowledge for forest conservation and mitigating human-wildlife conflict

title: Vacancies in key posts likely to impact TTD functioning
content: The position of Joint Executive Officer (Tirupati) is still vacant, while it has been reported that Sri Venkateswara Bhakti Channel, the TTD’s visual media arm, has lacked an administrator for quite some time now

title: Karnataka High Court dismisses actor Darshan’s plea against release of Kannada movie BOSS
content: Observing that cinematic portrayals, even if they bear similarities to real-life events, cannot influence or sway courts adjudicating criminal cases, the High Court of Karnataka dismissed actor Darshan’s plea to stop the release of Kannada film Boss

title: "Sonam, It's Done, Bro": Salman Khan Urges Wangchuk To End Fast, Asks Students To Go Back
content: Sonam Wangchuk has been on hunger strike since June 28, demanding accountability over paper leaks and reforms in the education system.

title: India Tests Kusha Long-Range Surface-To-Air Defence Missile
content: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh said it signifies a "giant leap" in air defence capability of the nation.

title: Rahul Gandhi, Opposition MPs Protest At Gandhi Smriti To Support Students
content: Rahul Gandhi said India's students are not alone and that the entire Opposition stands with them and their demands.

title: Mobile Internet Suspended In 1.5-km Radius Of Jantar Mantar Till Midnight
content: Section 163 has also been imposed outside Union Home Minister Amit Shah's residence, with police issuing warnings in the area.

title: Students' Concerns Genuine, Must Be Handled With Empathy: BJP Veteran MM Joshi
content: "I fervently hope that this is not treated merely as a law-and-order issue to be tackled through the use of force," former BJP chief Murli Manohar Joshi said

title: CJP Protest LIVE Updates: No Plan To Crack Down On Student Protest Tonight, Says Delhi Police
content: CJP Jantar Mantar protest LIVE: The Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) protesters are continuing their sit-in at Jantar Mantar.

title: How To Poison Someone, Burn Body: Bengaluru Man Asks AI Before Triple Murders
content: Investigators found that the accused had used Google Gemini to create hypothetical scenarios and seek information related to committing crimes.

title: Opinion: Opinion | A New US-Israel Defence Bill Has Opened An Unexpected Door For India
content: The phrase "integration of the US and Israeli militaries" has become popular because it captures attention. It also obscures the more important story.

title: US-Saudi Nuclear Agreement Hinges On Riyadh Recognising Israel: Trump
content: The deal to establish a civilian nuclear program in Saudi Arabia "will be approved, but is totally subject to Saudi Arabia joining the very respected and successful Abraham Accords," Trump said.

title: Secret Service Agent Suspended For 'Leaking' JD Vance's Travel Details
content: The agency's internal affairs component has opened an investigation into it; however, it is unclear if the agent could face administrative punishment or criminal charges.

title: Delhi University Rs 26,000, Allahabad Rs 3,000: Students Question Fee Disparity
content: "Can children of farmers like us afford such high fees? This is a fight for educational reform, and the government must take it seriously," a student at Jantar Mantar said

title: Man Sentenced To Death For Sexual Assault, Murder Of 7-Year-Old Boy In UP
content: According to the prosecution, the seven-year-old child went missing on May 12, 2026, from Dayalpurwa Majhara Taukli village under the Kaiserganj police station area.

title: Shops, Eateries At Delhi's Connaught Place Shut At 6.30 PM Amid CJP Protests
content: The advisory said all shop owners, office establishments and restaurants in the area have been urged to cooperate and strictly comply with the directions.

title: 13 Arrests, Multi-State Network: What NEET Leak Probe Has Revealed So Far
content: The investigation so far has revealed that specific questions from the Physics, Chemistry, and Biology sections of the NEET-UG 2026 exam had reached certain individuals before the test, which were then passed on to students in exchange for money.

title: "Here To Seek Justice": Students Protest In Guwahati Over Exam Paper Leaks
content: They urged comprehensive education reforms and said the minister should resign over what they described as repeated lapses in the examination system.

title: Death Count In Sikkim Tunnel Collapse Rises To 25, All Bodies Recovered
content: All the bodies have now been recovered after 72 hours of continuous search operations, K K Roy, a member of the rescue team from Eastern Coalfield Ltd, told reporters.

title: NDTV Datafy: What History Shows About Economic Impact Of Protests
content: Social unrest can disrupt economic activity by affecting consumer confidence, business sentiment and investor behaviour, according to IMF

title: UP polls: 'UP ke ladke' together again to take on BJP, but who will show 'bigger heart'?
content: Priyanka Mukherjee is an Assistant News Editor at The Times of India, where deadlines rarely slow down. Often juggling breaking news, long-form explainers and the everyday chaos of the newsroom, she is usually managing stress, chasing clarity and trying to log off on time (rarely successful). She also cares deeply about stories around women’s rights and social change, while two very opinionated dogs make sure she never truly switches off. Read More
